step_by_step_thinking:

[Waiting for data]
Retrieved Evidence:
Title: "Epidemiology of Sjögren's Syndrome"
Summary: This study reviews the global incidence rates and symptom prevalences among those diagnosed with Sjögren’s syndrome. Key findings include high incidences of xerostomia and keratoconjunctivitis, moderate frequencies of arthritis, and low occurrences of lymphomas compared to other rheumatologic conditions.
Analysis: After reviewing the retrieved evidence, there is strong support confirming that xerostomia (dry mouth), keratoconjunctivitis (dry eye), and arthritis are indeed prevalent symptoms in Sjogren's syndrome, whereas lymphoma appears much less commonly associated with the condition. Given this robust statistical backing highlighting how rarely lymphoma presents itself uniquely within Sjogren’s syndrome profiles, my stance remains aligned with the prior conclusions drawn.
